title: Prompt injection guard on trust boundary inputs (iPhone bridge + Notes inbox)
description: Mira already audits *skills* for malicious code via soul_manager.audit_skill(), but applies no analogous check to *incoming content* that reaches the LLM — specifically the iPhone/iCloud bridge messages and the Apple Notes inbox. These are legitimate, trusted channels (same as Stripe's emoji), but their content is externally authored and could carry prompt injection payloads. Add a soul_manager.check_prompt_injection(text) function that scans incoming text for canonical injection patterns ('ignore previous instructions', 'you are now', 'disregard', '###SYSTEM', etc.) before dispatch, and log + quarantine flagged messages rather than silently forwarding them to agents.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: legitimate features and trust relationships ARE the attack surface — not just suspicious external tools. Stripe's emoji trigger is the trust boundary; the vulnerability is that the emoji content (e.g. an issue comment) could be attacker-controlled. Mira's analogous trust boundaries are the iPhone bridge and Notes inbox: Mira treats messages arriving there as user-authoritative, but their content could be crafted by a third party (a website Mira visited, a shared note, a forwarded message). The skill audit guards one input path; the message channels are unguarded.
diff_description: 1. In soul_manager.py: add check_prompt_injection(text: str) -> tuple[bool, str] that tests for a configurable blocklist of injection phrases (case-insensitive regex) plus heuristics like >3 consecutive instruction-style sentences from a non-user source. Returns (is_flagged, reason). 2. In core.py, in the iPhone message dispatch path and the Apple Notes inbox handler: call check_prompt_injection() on the raw message body before passing to agent dispatch. If flagged: log to logs/security.log with the full message and reason, send a notification back to the iPhone app (quarantine notice), and skip dispatch. Do not silently drop — the user should know a message was blocked.
